items.summary: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

items.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
items.summary: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

items.title: Crows' skipper Jordan Dawson ruled out for Showdown after attending brother's funeral
items.summary: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

items.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
items.summary: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

items.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline since 2020, royal commission finds
items.summary: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

items.title: What is inflation and should you worry about it?
items.summary: The latest inflation data is making headlines. Find out what it means for you in three minutes.

items.title: Ross Lyon clarifies Saints resignation offer over comment to First Nations players
items.summary: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon is proud of the way the club reacted after he offended a group of the club's First Nations players.

items.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
items.summary: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

items.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
items.summary: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in primary school bathroom
items.summary: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

items.title: Bodycam footage shows police officers subduing London attacker
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?
items.summary: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

items.title: Police say 'timeframe of survivability' ending for missing 5yo Sharon
items.summary: After speaking to survival experts, NT police says the "timeframe of survivability" is coming to an end for a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs. The police commissioner also confirmed the search had been complicated by the fact the child is non-verbal.

items.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
items.summary: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

items.title: Travis Head helps Sunrisers score six-wicket IPL win over Indians
items.summary: Travis Head reminds cricket fans of what he can produce with the bat, as he helps Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket win over Mumbai Indians.

items.title: Australians urged to stand up for Murray River as 'transformational moment' looms
items.summary: Julie Jones has lived by the River Murray all her life and wants all South Australians to participate in the first comprehensive review of the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

items.title: ASX falls as oil price hits four-year high of $US126 — as it happened
items.summary: The Australian share market traded lower as the oil price surged above $US125 in afternoon trading. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Six WA Police officers sanctioned over treatment of Aboriginal man who died in Kalgoorlie prison
items.summary: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

items.title: War deepens oil crisis and pushes inflation higher — as it happened
items.summary: The price of Brent crude has surged to over $US120 per barrel because US President Donald Trump has announced he will extend the naval blockade of the strait.

items.title: New study suggests majority of regional Australians back renewable energy transition
items.summary: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

items.title: Technology should make life easier but it doesn't always for people with disability
items.summary: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

items.title: Will the OPEC oil cartel survive without the UAE?
items.summary: The United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for the past 66 years.

items.title: The vine with a murky past that is strangling Vanuatu's forests
items.summary: A habitat-destroying invasive species is taking over Vanuatu's forests, but one village is fighting back and hoping to earn an income for its people at the same time.

items.title: Matthew Pavlich and Steven Baker reflect on the infamous 'Sirengate' match in Launceston 20 years on
items.summary: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

items.title: Flag quiz, part 1: Are you a vexillologist in the making?
items.summary: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

items.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation

items.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
items.summary: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

items.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
items.summary: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

items.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

items.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
items.summary: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

items.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
items.summary: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

items.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
items.summary: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Inflation surges to highest level since 2023
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
items.summary: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

items.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
items.summary: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

items.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
items.summary: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

items.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
items.summary: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

items.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
items.summary: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

items.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
items.summary: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

items.title: AI deepfakes are circulating in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?
items.summary: Deepfake content, made from artificial intelligence, is increasingly circulating in schools. But what can parents do if their children are the target?

items.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
items.summary: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

items.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

items.title: Keith Lees faces court over alleged murder of Meaghan Louise Rose on Sunshine Coast in 1997
items.summary: Police allege Keith Lees killed Meaghan Louise Rose, whose body was found at the base of cliffs on the Sunshine Coast in July 1997.

items.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
items.summary: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

items.title: Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent in March as higher fuel prices bite
items.summary: Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent in March, but underlying inflation remains at 3.3 per cent.

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her phone to avoid handing over key texts as part of defamation trial
items.summary: Ms Wilson's lawyers have previously told Federal Court defamation hearing that her phone was stolen and she was unable to produce all of her WhatsApp message history.

items.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
items.summary: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.

items.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
items.summary: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

items.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
items.summary: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

items.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe
items.summary: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

items.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery says her death was preventable
items.summary: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

items.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
items.summary: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their major off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

items.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
items.summary: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

items.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
items.summary: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

items.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: UAE exit from OPEC another disruption to the fragile oil market
items.summary: The United Arab Emirates is set to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, creating yet another disruption to the global oil market. At home, Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the Middle East war is to blame for a rise in inflation.

items.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
items.summary: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

items.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
items.summary: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

items.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
items.summary: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

items.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
items.summary: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
items.summary: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

items.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
items.summary: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

items.title: Superyacht linked to Putin's friend slips through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: It's the most talked-about and dangerous stretch of ocean in the world but, at the weekend, a superyacht linked to one of Russia's richest men made it through the Strait of Hormuz.
